% Hand-edited in Phase 8: demoted to a subsection of the consolidated Qualitative Examples
% appendix, pruned to four of the ten sequences (the other six kept as comments below), and
% the caption shortened. Re-apply if this file is regenerated.

\subsection{Infill Recovery on Seeded Sequences}
\label{app:showcase-infill}
% The former \label{app:showcase} now lives on the consolidated section in 08_appendix.tex.
SELECTION POLICY. The ten sequences were drawn once with a fixed rule and never filtered: \texttt{np.random.\allowbreak default\_rng(0).\allowbreak choice(200,\allowbreak 10,\allowbreak replace=False)} over the kl-baselines set (WikiText-2 validation, one random interior mask, \texttt{data\_seed}~0), sorted. Four of those ten, the first, fourth, seventh and tenth of the sorted draw, are shown here; the remaining six are retained in the source of this document. Failures are kept in, and nothing is selected for quality. The corrupted and recovered token at the masked position is shown in bold; a check mark means the recovered token equals the ground truth. Numbers cross-reference Sections~\ref{sec:results-baselines} and~\ref{sec:results-diffusion}.
\begin{center}\footnotesize
\begin{longtable}{p{0.30\textwidth} p{0.60\textwidth} c}
\caption[Infill recovery on four seeded sequences.]{Infill recovery on four of the ten seeded sequences, all methods, on the reference sequence set.}\label{tab:showcase-infill}\\
\toprule Method & Recovered token (in context) & Hit\\ \midrule
\endfirsthead \toprule Method & Recovered token (in context) & Hit\\ \midrule \endhead \bottomrule \endfoot
\multicolumn{3}{l}{\textbf{Sequence idx 3} \quad ground truth: 'fish'\quad corrupted: 'moments'}\\
\multicolumn{3}{p{0.92\textwidth}}{\itshape original: The diet of H. gammarus mostly consists of other benthic invertebrates. These include crabs, molluscs, sea urchins, starfish and polychaete worms.}\\
\multicolumn{3}{p{0.92\textwidth}}{\itshape corrupted: The diet of H. gammarus mostly consists of other benthic invertebrates. These include crabs, molluscs, sea urchins, star\textbf{moments} and polychaete worms.}\\
\addlinespace[2pt]
DLS policy (MH, GN) & The diet of H. gammarus mostly consists of other benthic invertebrates. These include crabs, molluscs, sea urchins, star\textbf{passages} and polychaete worms. & \\
DLS random dir & The diet of H. gammarus mostly consists of other benthic invertebrates. These include crabs, molluscs, sea urchins, star\textbf{fossils} and polychaete worms. & \\
CLS flagship (policy, MH, GN, free, s50) & The diet of H. gammarus mostly consists of other benthic invertebrates. These include crabs, molluscs, sea urchins, star\textbf{specialization} and polychaete worms. & \\
Metropolized Gibbs & The diet of H. gammarus mostly consists of other benthic invertebrates. These include crabs, molluscs, sea urchins, star\textbf{fish} and polychaete worms. & \checkmark\\
Conditional argmax & The diet of H. gammarus mostly consists of other benthic invertebrates. These include crabs, molluscs, sea urchins, star\textbf{fish} and polychaete worms. & \checkmark\\
Conditional top-k rescore & The diet of H. gammarus mostly consists of other benthic invertebrates. These include crabs, molluscs, sea urchins, star\textbf{fish} and polychaete worms. & \checkmark\\
Left-conditional (independence MH) & The diet of H. gammarus mostly consists of other benthic invertebrates. These include crabs, molluscs, sea urchins, star\textbf{fish} and polychaete worms. & \checkmark\\
SEDD-small recovery & The diet of H. gammarus mostly consists of other benthic invertebrates. These include crabs, molluscs, sea urchins, star\textbf{lings} and polychaete worms. & \\
SEDD-medium recovery & The diet of H. gammarus mostly consists of other benthic invertebrates. These include crabs, molluscs, sea urchins, star\textbf{lings} and polychaete worms. & \\
Hybrid (SEDD proposal, exact-energy MH) & The diet of H. gammarus mostly consists of other benthic invertebrates. These include crabs, molluscs, sea urchins, star\textbf{fish} and polychaete worms. & \checkmark\\
\midrule
